calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-20-2025 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-13-2025 Continued To Nov-12-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Raymond Raymond 415-660-8980 raymsqrd@gmail.com Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Damon Thurnston, Esq. 510-433-2600 Rankin Shuey Mintz Lampasona & Harper for Defendant.

Argued. The Court indicates a late notice of contest is received from Plaintiff. Mr. Raymond presents arguments; a checklist of 4 items. Mr. Thurnston stands on his briefing and has no oral argument. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JAMES SENAL DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant James Senal's Demurrer to the Amended Complaint is Taken Under Submsission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (see scanned order for details)***

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2025 Continued To Sep-10-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2025 Continued To Aug-05-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JAMES SENAL DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Continued to August 5, 2025, on the court's motion.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-09-2025 Continued To Jul-30-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
No Appearance by Plaintiff
DAMON M. THURSTON, ESQ. (510) 433-2600 RANKIN, SHUEY, MINTZ, LAMPASONA & HARPER for Defendant.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court indicates it received a form of contest from the Plaintiff Raymond Raymond. At 9:21am, there is no Apearance by the Plaintiff in Open Court. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT JAMES SENAL DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant Senal's demurrer to the complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend.

The court grants defendants request for judicial notice. The court may take judicial notice of state court records under Evidence Code section 452(d)(1).

Judicially noticeable documents and the complaint indicate that this attorney malpractice action is time barred. CCP 340.6(a) states: "[a]n action against an attorney for a wrongful act or omission, other than for actual fraud, arising in the performance of professional services shall be commenced within one year after the plaintiff discovers, or through the use of reasonable diligence should have discovered, the facts constituting the wrongful act or omission, or four years from the date of the wrongful act or omission, whichever occurs first."

Plaintiff alleges that defendant's legal work fell below the standard of care. (Complaint, 3:11-24.) Defendant withdrew from representing plaintiff on April 10, 2018. (Thurston Decl., Ex. 33.) The complaint also references plaintiff's release from incarceration in 2019. (Complaint, 3:6.) The reasonable inference from this record is that plaintiff should have been on notice of the allegedly deficient representation by the date of release, at the latest December 31, 2019. Plaintiff filed this action on August 30, 2024, and it therefore appears time barred.

Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead facts showing tolling from the statute of limitations or some other basis why his claim is timely. (Code Civil Procedure section 340.6(a)(1)-(5).)

The court also reminds plaintiff to plead his claim by presenting a "statement of facts...in ordinary and concise language." (Code Civil Procedure section 425.10(a)(1).) The complaint is presently unclear regarding how defendant allegedly breached his duty of care and ineffectively represented plaintiff.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, FTR (9:21:11 - 9:24:21)Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2025 Continued To May-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Mr. Raymond was in the courtroom as an observer and did not check in with the clerk.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JAMES SENAL DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant James Senal's demurrer to Plaintiff Raymond Raymond's complaint is CONTINUED TO MAY 7, 2025, AT 9 A.M. IN THIS DEPARTMENT; PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION/RESPONSE TO THE DEMURRER MUST BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN APRIL 21, 2025, WITH A COURTESY COPY PROVIDED TO THIS DEPARTMENT WITHIN 24 HOURS OF FILING; DEFENDANT'S REPLY DUE APRIL 29, 2025, WITH A COURTESY COPY PROVIDED TO THIS DEPARTMENT WITHIN 24 HOURS OF FILING.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
